DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, AS ASSIGNEE OF THE FDIC AS RECEIVER OF UNITED COMMERCIAL BANK

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF EAST WEST BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO PARAGRAPH 20 OF THE DECLARATION OF MS. SHIVERS-WYNN ARE SUSTAINED. BECAUSE THE RECORDS THAT PURPORTEDLY PROVIDE THE BASES FOR PLAINTIFF'S CALCULATION OF ITS DAMAGES ARE NEITHER ATTACHED NOR SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFIED, THE DECLARANT'S TESTIMONY ABOUT THOSE RECORDS LACKS FOUNDATION AND VIOLATES THE SECONDARY EVIDENCE RULE. THIS IS PARTICULARLY TRUE AS TO ASSERTED DAMAGES SUCH AS "ESCROW ADVANCES" THAT VERY LIKELY DID NOT ORIGINATE WITH PLAINTIFF OR ITS PREDECESSOR. ONCE THIS OBJECTION IS SUSTAINED, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE REQUIRED ELEMENT OF DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Dec-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff's First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Second Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents is off calendar per the Re-Notice filed on November 10, 2012. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-06-2012 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-12-2012 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-25-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Thomas P. Byrne, Esq. of the Law Office of Thomas P. Byrne, Tel. 415.533.3636, appearing for defendant and opposing party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EAST WEST BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or, alternatively, summary adjudication is Denied in its entirety. Defendants' objections to paragraph 20 of the declaration of Ms. Shivers-Wynn are sustained. Because the records that purportedly provide the bases for plaintiff's calculation of its damages are neither attached nor specifically identified, the declarant's testimony about those records lacks foundation and violates the secondary evidence rule. This is particularly true as to asserted damages such as "escrow advances" that very likely did not originate with plaintiff or its predecessor. Once this objection is sustained, plaintiff has not met its burden of presenting evidence to support the required element of damages. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
